introSection: GateKeeper is a steel winged roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It is one of the largest Wing Coasters built by Bolliger & Mabillard. Both Disaster Transport and Space Spiral (an Observation Tower) were removed from the park in order to make room for the ride. The attraction opened on May 11, 2013.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
GateKeeper was officially announced on August 13, 2012, shortly after the demolition of Disaster Transport commenced.

In October 2012, the first track pieces arrived at the park. The ride went vertical in November 2012. Later that month, the lift hill was topped off. In January 2013, the first keyhole structure was built. The layout was completed in February 2013.

The lead car from one of GateKeeper's trains was on show at Bolliger & Mabillard's booth at the 2013 IAAPA Expo.

Testing of GateKeeper began in April 2013.

The ride opened on May 11, 2013.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
GateKeeper was the fastest and tallest Bolliger & Mabillard Wing Coaster when it opened. The coaster itself is described as a "front gate statement— a roller coaster that flies overhead, rolls and flies back— highly visible above guests entering the park."

There are two "keyhole" elements — narrow slots which the coaster passes through — which form part of the new entrance to Cedar Point.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Blue and cyan track and white supports.

sections.title: Ride Experience
sections.content: The train is hoisted up a lift hill. Once riders crest the top of the lift hill, the train will rotate 180 degrees to the right and the train will fall down the drop, reaching speeds of. The roller coaster then rolls through an adjacent immelmann, and then coasts over a camelback hill. This is followed by a "giant flat spin" and through a zero-g roll—which includes two key-hole towers. At the bottom of the zero-g roll, the train coasts through an inclined dive loop, then through an inline roll adjacent to the keyhole towers. The train continues through a mid-course brake-run, a camelback drop, a 360° helix, a small bunny hop and then into the main brakes.

sections.title: Incident
sections.content: On June 12, 2021, the lift chain broke causing a train to become stuck on the lift hill. Riders had to be evacuated via the stairs adjacent to the lift hill. GateKeeper is closed to allow the chain to be replaced. It reopened toward the end of July.

fullText:
GateKeeper is a steel winged roller coaster located at Cedar Point in Sandusky, Ohio, USA. It is one of the largest Wing Coasters built by Bolliger & Mabillard. Both Disaster Transport and Space Spiral (an Observation Tower) were removed from the park in order to make room for the ride. The attraction opened on May 11, 2013.

GateKeeper was officially announced on August 13, 2012, shortly after the demolition of Disaster Transport commenced.

In October 2012, the first track pieces arrived at the park. The ride went vertical in November 2012. Later that month, the lift hill was topped off. In January 2013, the first keyhole structure was built. The layout was completed in February 2013.

The lead car from one of GateKeeper's trains was on show at Bolliger & Mabillard's booth at the 2013 IAAPA Expo.

Testing of GateKeeper began in April 2013.

The ride opened on May 11, 2013.

GateKeeper was the fastest and tallest Bolliger & Mabillard Wing Coaster when it opened. The coaster itself is described as a "front gate statement— a roller coaster that flies overhead, rolls and flies back— highly visible above guests entering the park."

There are two "keyhole" elements — narrow slots which the coaster passes through — which form part of the new entrance to Cedar Point.

Blue and cyan track and white supports.



The train is hoisted up a lift hill. Once riders crest the top of the lift hill, the train will rotate 180 degrees to the right and the train will fall down the drop, reaching speeds of. The roller coaster then rolls through an adjacent immelmann, and then coasts over a camelback hill. This is followed by a "giant flat spin" and through a zero-g roll—which includes two key-hole towers. At the bottom of the zero-g roll, the train coasts through an inclined dive loop, then through an inline roll adjacent to the keyhole towers. The train continues through a mid-course brake-run, a camelback drop, a 360° helix, a small bunny hop and then into the main brakes.

On June 12, 2021, the lift chain broke causing a train to become stuck on the lift hill. Riders had to be evacuated via the stairs adjacent to the lift hill. GateKeeper is closed to allow the chain to be replaced. It reopened toward the end of July.
